36.reason: This line incorrectly prints the surrounding characters, causing duplication. Only the current dash should be printed when no expansion occurs.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  This prints the three characters again, but the loop already printed str[i-1] and str[i+1] in the else branch of the outer loop, causing duplication. For "B-e", when i=1, str[i-1]='B' was already printed in the previous iteration, and str[i+1]='e' will be printed later, so this causes "BB-ee" output.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  This prints the characters around the dash again, but the loop already printed str[i-1] in the previous iteration (since i-1 was printed as a non-dash character). This causes duplication of the character before the dash.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Should only print the hyphen (str[i]) because the left and right characters are printed in their own iterations; printing all three causes duplicates.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  This line incorrectly prints the surrounding characters, causing duplication. Only the current dash should be printed when no expansion occurs.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  This causes double printing. The characters str[i-1] and str[i+1] are already printed (or will be printed) in their respective loop iterations. For example, str[i-1] was printed when i was i-1. str[i+1] will be printed when i is i+1. Printing them here again results in duplication. Instead, only the '-' should be printed if no expansion occurs, or the logic should skip printing the neighbors in their own iterations. Given the structure, simply printing '-' is not enough if we don't adjust neighbors. A common fix is to only print the current character str[i] (which is '-') in the else case, and rely on the loop to print the neighbors. However, if we just print '-', the output for B-e would be B-e (from i=0:'B', i=1:'-', i=2:'e'). This matches expected. So replace this line with printf("%c", str[i]);
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Logical Error] This prints the previous character, the dash, and the next character. However, the previous character was already printed in the previous iteration (i-1), and the next character will be printed in the next iteration (i+1). This leads to duplicate output. It should only print the dash character `str[i]` (or just '-') to represent the unexpanded symbol, as the neighbors are handled independently by the loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // This prints three characters at once, but in the loop, each character is normally printed individually. This causes duplication: e.g., for "B-e", when i=1 ('-'), it prints "B-e", but then when i=0 and i=2 are processed separately, 'B' and 'e' get printed again, leading to "BB-ee".
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This prints three characters at once when '-' is encountered but doesn't belong to a valid expansion, causing duplication when adjacent '-' are processed or when non-expansion cases appear. For example, in "B-e", when i points to '-', it prints 'B', '-', 'e'. But the characters before and after '-' will also be printed again when i moves to those positions, leading to duplicated output like "BB-ee".]
